relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Joseph was brought down to Egypt and sold into Potiphar’s house.
Even in descent, the Eternal was with Joseph and made him successful.
Potiphar saw that blessing moved through Joseph’s hand.
Favor lifted Joseph into responsibility over the house.
Because of Joseph, blessing spread through house and field.
Everything was trusted to Joseph, and his beauty was noticed.
His master’s woman turned desire toward him.
Joseph refused, remembering trust and responsibility.
He named the act as betrayal and sin against the Divine.
Day after day, he would not listen to the invitation.
A lonely moment came inside the house.
She seized his garment, but Joseph fled, leaving cloth behind rather than integrity.
The abandoned garment became the tool of accusation.
She recast Joseph as a threat before the household.
The story was shaped around the garment he left behind.
She kept the garment until Potiphar came.
She told Potiphar the accusation.
The garment was made to witness falsely.
Potiphar’s anger burned.
Joseph was placed in prison.
Yet the Eternal was with him there too, extending covenant-kindness and favor.
Joseph became trusted even among prisoners.
The prison chief trusted Joseph because the Eternal made his work prosper.
